Headline: Merger - Beaudesert Park School, Gloucestershire
Info: Beaudesert Park School, an independent prep school, has announced it will merge with the Rugby School Group in July 2026, securing a seven-figure investment to enhance its site and facilities.

Headline: Closure - Middle Street Primary (State), Brighton
Info: Brighton and Hove City Council is advancing proposals to close Middle Street Primary School due to a severe financial deficit and falling pupil numbers, whilst an independent investigation into the school's governance has been ordered.

Headline: Closure - LIPA Primary and High School (State), Liverpool
Info: Parents are campaigning to save the arts-focused LIPA Primary and High School from closure, as the institution enters the final stages of a consultation period driven by financial concerns and falling pupil numbers [3.8].

Headline: News - Monkseaton Middle School (State), North Tyneside
Info: Monkseaton Middle School has been saved from closure following a council consultation and will instead expand its year groups to secure its financial future.

Headline: Restructuring - Bede's School, Eastbourne
Info: Bede's School has announced a strategic expansion to launch a new Upper School, offering a streamlined day-school alternative with lower fees in response to the evolving economic landscape of independent education.

Headline: Merger - Amesbury School, Surrey
Info: Historic Amesbury School in Hindhead has announced it will merge with the Charterhouse family of schools on 1 August to secure its long-term future and enrich pupil opportunities. [3.2]

Headline: Financial - King Edward's School, Bath
Info: King Edward's School is selling its Weston Lane campus to raise vital funds and consolidate its operations onto a single site by 2027, citing financial pressures from the imposition of VAT on fees.

Headline: Closure Averted - Monkseaton Middle School (State), North Tyneside
Info: Monkseaton Middle School, which was faced with the prospect of closure due to low birth rates and budget deficits, has had its closure put on hold following a council consultation and plans to expand its year groups.

Headline: Financial - Private Special Schools, National
Info: An investigation has revealed that councils are facing unsustainable financial pressures, spending up to four times as much per pupil on private special schools compared to state equivalents. In response, the government is preparing to implement new funding bands to rein in these escalating costs.

Headline: Financial - Settle College (State), North Yorkshire
Info: A controversial home-to-school transport policy is contributing to severe financial pressures and falling pupil numbers at Settle College. Local councillors have raised concerns about the state school's future viability if the demographic and financial impacts continue.

Headline: Merger - Beaudesert Park School, Gloucestershire
Info: Beaudesert Park School, a highly regarded independent prep school, has announced it will merge with the historic Rugby School Group from July 2026, benefiting from a seven-figure investment in its site and facilities [6.17].
